title: ERD Views — Save, Load, and Share Custom Diagram Perspectives
story: As a DataTools Pro user working with the Entity Relationship Diagram, I want to selectively hide objects and save those selections as named views, so that I can create focused diagram perspectives without changing which objects are in my data model.
description:
Adds the ability to create, save, and load named ERD views that control which objects are visible in the diagram. This is independent of which objects are managed in the user's data model — a view simply toggles visibility of objects on the diagram.

A dropdown defaults to "All Objects" and allows users to load saved views. An "Edit Views" button opens a side panel listing all model objects as checkboxes. Unchecking an object hides it from the diagram. Users can name and save the current checkbox state as a view, and views can be marked as shared (team-level, not per-user).

Edge cases handled:
- If an object is removed from the model after being saved in a view, the view gracefully handles the missing object rather than breaking.
- If a new object is added to the model after a view is saved, it defaults to unchecked (hidden) when that view is loaded.
- The "showUserRelationships" URL parameter is respected in the side panel checkbox list, keeping counts consistent between the diagram and the panel.

The query layer was refactored from raw SQL to Laravel Eloquent models for maintainability.
acceptance_criteria: ERD page displays a dropdown defaulting to 'All Objects' that lists saved views
Clicking 'Edit Views' opens a right-side panel with checkboxes for all active model objects
Unchecking an object in the panel immediately hides it from the diagram
User can save the current checkbox state as a named view
User can rename an existing saved view via a pencil icon
Views can be marked as shared at the team level
If an object is removed from the model, saved views referencing it do not break
Newly added objects default to unchecked when loading a pre-existing view
Object count in the side panel matches the diagram count when showUserRelationships is toggled
Selecting a view from the main dropdown or the side panel dropdown synchronizes both controls
Permission settings control who can create and manage ERD views
ux_changes: New dropdown on ERD page for selecting saved views. 'Edit Views' button opens a right-side panel with object checkboxes. Pencil icon for renaming views, plus icon for creating new views. Diagram updates in real time as checkboxes are toggled.
middleware_changes: Refactored ERD object retrieval from raw SQL to Laravel Eloquent models (ObjectField model with join on Scorecards). Added view CRUD logic including save, update, and load operations scoped to team_id. Added showUserRelationships parameter handling in AJAX calls.
data_model_changes: New ERD views table to store view metadata (name, team_id, shared flag) and the set of visible/hidden objects per view.
api_changes: AJAX endpoints for creating, updating, loading, and listing ERD views. Checkbox state changes trigger real-time updates to both the diagram and the view record.
implementation_notes: The query was refactored from raw DB::select() with string concatenation to ObjectField::select() with Eloquent joins and where clauses. The showUserRelationships URL parameter must be parsed and forwarded in the AJAX call that populates the side panel checkboxes to ensure consistent object counts. Views are saved at the team_id scope. Permission settings were added for ERD view management.
comment_summary: Extensive back-and-forth clarifying requirements. Key decisions: views are shared at team level (not per-user), checkbox panel should show all active (non-soft-deleted) model objects, and newly added objects default to hidden when loading existing views. Developer refactored raw SQL to Laravel Eloquent and added permission settings.
